method: Outside-in crawl (browser UA, robots-respecting — nyc.gov robots.txt only disallows /html/misc/). Fingerprinted the nyc.gov Livesite CMS from response headers; confirmed the TLC Trip Record Data parquet host (d37ci6vzurychx.cloudfront.net/trip-data, HEAD 200, ~50MB, AmazonS3) as a bulk file host, not an API. Enumerated NYC Open Data via the Socrata catalog API and verified the agency label 'Taxi and Limousine Commission (TLC)' → 80 datasets pulled with columns.
headline_findings: TLC is the most DATA-rich domain in the project and the least API-native: a world-famous open-data producer whose data ships as batch, not as a queryable API.

The flagship TLC Trip Record Data (yellow/green/FHV/FHVHV) is monthly PARQUET files on CloudFront/S3 (d37ci6vzurychx.cloudfront.net/trip-data) — a bulk download host, NOT an API. Only a subset of yearly trip snapshots is mirrored on Socrata.

80 TLC datasets on NYC Open Data (agency 'Taxi and Limousine Commission (TLC)', verified) cover licensed drivers, vehicles, medallion/SHL permits, FHV bases, inspection schedules, and application status — but as flat daily snapshots, one dataset per license class.
The Taxi Zones behind every trip's PULocationID/DOLocationID are a static CSV + shapefile (/misc/taxi_zone_lookup.csv, taxi_zones.zip), not a resource.
Licensing exposes only READ-ONLY status (TLC New Driver Application Status dpec-ucu7); there is no API to APPLY for a TLC license. That is the one net-new write surface.
Website runs on the shared NYC.gov Livesite CMS (nginx + Akamai + AWS ALB + Dynatrace) — a brochure; none of TLC's operational data is served from it. See tech-stack.md and apis-observed.md.

apis_observed.note: TLC Trip Record Data — monthly parquet per service class; download host, not an API. Verified 200, ~50MB, AmazonS3.
taxi_zone_lookup.csv + taxi_zones.zip — the ~263 zones behind trip LocationIDs.
80 TLC datasets; flat daily snapshots; no unified TLC resource model.
used to verify the agency label + enumerate datasets.
issues licenses; only read-only status leaks to Open Data.
brochure content; no content API; Dynatrace RUM.

fruit.name: TLC Trip Record Data (yellow/green/FHV/FHVHV)
fruit.notes: The flagship dataset — monthly parquet on CloudFront/S3, not queryable. Pickup/drop-off are TaxiZone LocationIDs.

fruit.name: TLC Taxi Zones
fruit.notes: The join key behind trip-record PULocationID/DOLocationID — published only as static files, not a resource.

fruit.name: For-Hire Vehicles (FHV) - Active
fruit.notes: Most-viewed TLC dataset (3.8M views). Licensed vehicles across FHV, medallion, SHL — one dataset per class.

fruit.name: Licensed Drivers (Medallion / FHV / SHL)
opendata_match: Medallion Drivers – Passenger Assistance Trained td5q-ry6d (7c)
fruit.notes: Licensed drivers as flat daily snapshots, one dataset per license class.

fruit.name: FHV / SHL Bases
fruit.notes: Dispatch bases with geocoded address + geography spine; monthly dispatched-trip aggregates in a separate dataset.

fruit.name: Medallion Inspection Schedule
opendata_match: Medallion Taxi Initial Inspection Schedule sp7n-275u (9c)
fruit.notes: Up to three scheduled inspection appointments per medallion at a DMV facility.

fruit.name: Apply for a TLC driver / vehicle license
opendata_match: TLC New Driver Application Status dpec-ucu7 (12c)
fruit.notes: Open Data exposes only read-only application STATUS + a requirement checklist (drug test, defensive driving, exam, WAV, medical). There is no API to submit an application — the one net-new write surface for this domain.

fruit.name: TLC Local Law 28 Report
fruit.notes: Aggregated Local Law 28 statutory reporting data.

fruit.name: TLC Local Law 31 Report
fruit.notes: Aggregated Local Law 31 statutory reporting data.
